file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, dev]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      run_e2e_tests:
        type: boolean
        default: false
        description: 'Run E2E tests (triggered by Inspector)'

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  fast-gate:
    name: Fast Gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    env:
      DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL || 'mongodb://localhost:27017/test' }}
      BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN }}
      PAYLOAD_SECRET: ${{ secrets.PAYLOAD_SECRET || 'test-secret-for-ci' }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
<<<<<<< HEAD
        with:
          version: 10.33.0
=======
>>>>>>> origin/dev

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - name: Verify DATABASE_URL is set
        run: |
          if [ -n "$DATABASE_URL" ]; then
            echo "DATABASE_URL is set: true"
          else
            echo "DATABASE_URL is set: false"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Security audit
        run: pnpm audit --audit-level=high || true # npm retired the audit endpoint as of 2025

      - name: Run typecheck
        run: pnpm typecheck

      - name: Run ESLint
        run: pnpm lint

      - name: Check formatting with Prettier
        run: pnpm format:check

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: pnpm test:unit -- --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage report
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-unit
          path: coverage/
          retention-days: 3

      - name: Check for TODO/FIXME in production code
        run: |
          if grep -r "TODO\|FIXME" src/ --exclude-dir=node_modules --exclude-dir=.next 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "⚠️  Found TODO/FIXME comments in production code"
          fi
        continue-on-error: true

  integration-tests:
    name: Integration Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [fast-gate]
    timeout-minutes: 15
    env:
      DATABASE_URL: mongodb://localhost:27017/test
      PAYLOAD_SECRET: ${{ secrets.PAYLOAD_SECRET || 'test-secret-for-ci' }}
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY || '' }}
      DEFAULT_TENANT_SLUG: default
      BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN }}

    services:
      mongodb:
        image: mongo:7
        ports:
          - 27017:27017
        options: >-
          --health-cmd "mongosh --eval 'db.runCommand({ ping: 1 })'"
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s

file.content:
{
  "landing": {
    "hero": {
      "badge": "Your digital math teacher",
      "title": "Learn Math the Smart Way",
      "subtitle": "Interactive lessons, guided practice, instant answers to your questions, and exam preparation — all in one place.",
      "cta": "Start Learning",
      "secondaryCta": "Explore Courses"
    },
    "features": {
      "heading": "Everything You Need to Succeed",
      "subheading": "Four powerful tools designed to help you master math at your own pace",
      "study": {
        "title": "Study",
        "description": "Structured lessons that break down complex topics into clear, digestible steps. Learn at your own pace with interactive content."
      },
      "practice": {
        "title": "Practice",
        "description": "Targeted exercises that reinforce what you've learned. Get instant feedback and track your progress over time."
      },
      "ask": {
        "title": "Ask",
        "description": "Stuck on a problem? Ask your AI teacher for help. Get step-by-step explanations tailored to your level."
      },
      "test": {
        "title": "Test",
        "description": "Prepare for exams with realistic practice tests. Identify your strengths and areas that need more work."
      }
    },
    "howItWorks": {
      "heading": "Get Started in 3 Steps",
      "steps": {
        "choose": {
          "title": "Choose Your Course",
          "description": "Select your grade level and course to get a personalized learning path."
        },
        "learn": {
          "title": "Learn & Practice",
          "description": "Work through lessons, solve exercises, and ask questions when you need help."
        },
        "master": {
          "title": "Master & Test",
          "description": "Track your progress, take practice tests, and build confidence for exams."
        }
      }
    },

82	 * Helper to check if user is admin
83	 * Safely handles union type (User | PayloadMcpApiKey)
84	 */
85	function isAdminUser(req: PayloadRequest): boolean {
86	  const user = req.user
87	  if (!user) return false
88	  // PayloadMcpApiKey doesn't have 'role', check collection first
89	  if ('collection' in user && user.collection === 'users' && user.role === 'admin') {
90	    return true
91	  }
92	  return false
93	}

401	    const defaultTenantSlug = process.env.DEFAULT_TENANT_SLUG || 'default'
402	    const existingTenant = await payload.find({
403	      collection: 'tenants',
404	      where: { slug: { equals: defaultTenantSlug } },
405	      limit: 1,
406	      overrideAccess: true,
407	    })
408	
409	    if (existingTenant.totalDocs === 0) {
410	      await payload.create({
411	        collection: 'tenants',
412	        data: {
413	          name: 'Default',
414	          slug: defaultTenantSlug,
415	          status: 'active',
416	        },
417	        overrideAccess: true,
418	      })
419	      payload.logger.info(`[onInit] Created default tenant "${defaultTenantSlug}"`)
420	    }
421	
422	    await runBackfillOnInit(payload)
423	    await runPopulateLessonBlocksOnInit(payload)
424	    await runLocalizeTeacherProfilesOnInit(payload)
425	    await seedTeacherProfiles(payload)
426	  },
427	})

289	  /**
290	   * Poll the duplication record until status is no longer 'running' or 'pending'.
291	   * Returns the final record state.
292	   */
293	  async function pollUntilDone(recordId: string): Promise<{
294	    status: string
295	    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
296	    failures: any[]
297	
298	    outputLesson?: string | null
299	    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
300	    outputExercises?: any[]
301	  }> {
302	    let record = await payload.findByID({
303	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
304	      id: recordId,
305	      depth: 0,
306	      overrideAccess: true,
307	    })
308	    const deadline = Date.now() + 30000
309	    while ((record.status === 'running' || record.status === 'pending') && Date.now() < deadline) {
310	      await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 500))
311	      record = await payload.findByID({
312	        collection: 'lesson-duplications',
313	        id: recordId,
314	        depth: 0,
315	        overrideAccess: true,
316	      })
317	    }
318	    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
319	    return record as any
320	  }

322	  it('5-exercise lesson with one forced failure ends in needs_review with failures recorded', async () => {
323	    // Create pending duplication record
324	    const record = await payload.create({
325	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
326	      data: { sourceLesson: sourceLessonId, level: 'medium', status: 'pending' },
327	      overrideAccess: true,
328	    })
329	    cleanupDuplicationIds.push(record.id)
330	
331	    expect(record.status).toBe('pending')
332	
333	    // Run orchestrator (mocked runStrategy forces failure on exercise containing '-3')
334	    try {
335	      await runDuplicationOrchestrator(record.id, payload)
336	    } catch {
337	      // Orchestrator may throw if the mock is not properly applied;
338	      // we still verify the DB record state below
339	    }
340	
341	    // Poll until done
342	    const finalRecord = await pollUntilDone(record.id)
343	
344	    // Final status must be needs_review (not succeeded, not failed)
345	    expect(finalRecord.status).toBe('needs_review')
346	
347	    // At least one failure entry expected (exercise containing '-3' threw in runStrategy)
348	    // Note: the exact count depends on exercise ID strings; we check ≥1 to be robust
349	    expect(finalRecord.failures).toBeDefined()
350	    expect(finalRecord.failures.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)
351	    expect(finalRecord.failures[0].code).toBe('GENERATION_FAILED')
352	    expect(finalRecord.failures[0].suggestedAction).toBe('skip')
353	
354	    // After orchestrator runs, outputLesson should be created
355	    // Note: outputExercises remain empty in this test because the runStrategy mock
356	    // bypasses processExercise (which calls createOutputExercise). Exercise creation
357	    // is verified in lesson-duplication-review-resolve.int.spec.ts instead.
358	    expect(finalRecord.outputLesson).toBeTruthy()
359	  }, 180000)
360	
361	  it('orchestrator does not abort when one exercise fails — remaining exercises are processed', async () => {
362	    // Create fresh pending record
363	    const record = await payload.create({
364	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
365	      data: { sourceLesson: sourceLessonId, level: 'medium', status: 'pending' },
366	      overrideAccess: true,
367	    })
368	    cleanupDuplicationIds.push(record.id)
369	
370	    // Run orchestrator; it may throw if the mock isn't applied (that's ok for this test)
371	    try {
372	      await runDuplicationOrchestrator(record.id, payload)
373	    } catch {
374	      // ignore
375	    }
376	
377	    const finalRecord = await pollUntilDone(record.id)
378	
379	    // The orchestrator should reach needs_review (not 'failed'), meaning it processed
380	    // exercises and collected failures rather than crashing completely.
381	    // If the orchestrator crashed early, status would be 'failed' with 0 failures.
382	    expect(finalRecord.status).toBe('needs_review')
383	
384	    // At least one failure means at least one exercise failed and the orchestrator
385	    // continued processing other exercises (did not abort on first failure).
386	    expect(finalRecord.failures).toBeDefined()
387	    expect(finalRecord.failures.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)
388	    expect(finalRecord.failures[0].code).toBe('GENERATION_FAILED')
389	
390	    // outputLesson should be created even when some exercises fail
391	    expect(finalRecord.outputLesson).toBeTruthy()
392	  }, 180000)
393	})

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Stripe Webhook Handler
3	 *
4	 * POST /api/webhooks/stripe
5	 *
6	 * Verifies Stripe webhook signature, updates Transaction status, and grants
7	 * entitlements on successful payment. Always returns 200 to satisfy Stripe's
8	 * requirement for fast acknowledgment.
9	 *
10	 * @fileType api-route
11	 * @domain payments
12	 * @pattern webhook
13	 * @ai-summary Handles Stripe webhook events for payment confirmation and refunds
14	 */
15	
16	import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
17	import Stripe from 'stripe'
18	
19	import { getPayload } from 'payload'
20	import config from '@payload-config'
21	
22	import { grantProductEntitlements } from '@/lib/payment/grant-entitlements'
23	import { verifyStripeWebhook } from '@/lib/payment/stripe'
24	
25	export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
26	  const payload = await getPayload({ config })
27	
28	  // 1. Extract raw body as Buffer for signature verification
29	  const arrayBuffer = await request.arrayBuffer()
30	  const rawBody = Buffer.from(arrayBuffer)
31	
32	  // 2. Extract signature header
33	  const signature = request.headers.get('stripe-signature')
34	  if (!signature) {
35	    payload.logger.error('Missing stripe-signature header')
36	    return NextResponse.json({ received: true }, { status: 200 })
37	  }
38	
39	  // 3. Verify webhook signature
40	  let event: Stripe.Event
41	  try {
42	    event = await verifyStripeWebhook(rawBody, signature)
43	  } catch (err) {
44	    payload.logger.error({ error: err }, 'Stripe webhook signature verification failed')
45	    return NextResponse.json({ received: true }, { status: 200 })
46	  }
47	
48	  // 4. Route event by type
49	  try {
50	    await handleEvent(payload, event)
51	  } catch (err) {
52	    payload.logger.error({ error: err, eventType: event.type }, 'Stripe webhook handler error')
53	  }
54	
55	  return NextResponse.json({ received: true }, { status: 200 })
56	}
57	
58	async function handleEvent(
59	  payload: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getPayload>>,
60	  event: Stripe.Event,
61	): Promise<void> {
62	  switch (event.type) {
63	    case 'checkout.session.completed': {
64	      const sessionId = event.data.object.id
65	
66	      // Find the transaction by providerTransactionId (Stripe session ID)
67	      const transactions = await payload.find({
68	        collection: 'transactions',
69	        where: {
70	          providerTransactionId: { equals: sessionId },
71	        },
72	        limit: 1,
73	        depth: 0,
74	        overrideAccess: true,
75	      })
76	
77	      if (transactions.totalDocs === 0) {
78	        payload.logger.warn({ sessionId }, 'Stripe webhook: transaction not found')
79	        return
80	      }
81	
82	      const transaction = transactions.docs[0]
83	
84	      // Idempotency: skip if already succeeded
85	      if (transaction.status === 'succeeded') {
86	        return
87	      }
88	
89	      // Update status to succeeded
90	      await payload.update({
91	        collection: 'transactions',
92	        id: transaction.id,
93	        data: { status: 'succeeded' },
94	        overrideAccess: true,
95	      })
96	
97	      // Grant entitlements
98	      try {
99	        await grantProductEntitlements(
100	          transaction.user as string,
101	          transaction.product as string,
102	          transaction.id,
103	        )
104	      } catch (err) {
105	        payload.logger.error(
106	          { error: err, transactionId: transaction.id },
107	          'Failed to grant product entitlements',
108	        )
109	      }
110	      break
111	    }
112	
113	    case 'checkout.session.expired': {
114	      const sessionId = event.data.object.id
115	
116	      const transactions = await payload.find({
117	        collection: 'transactions',
118	        where: {
119	          providerTransactionId: { equals: sessionId },
120	        },
121	        limit: 1,
122	        depth: 0,
123	        overrideAccess: true,
124	      })
125	
126	      if (transactions.totalDocs === 0) {
127	        payload.logger.warn(
128	          { sessionId },
129	          'Stripe webhook: transaction not found for session.expired',
130	        )
131	        return
132	      }
133	
134	      await payload.update({
135	        collection: 'transactions',
136	        id: transactions.docs[0].id,
137	        data: { status: 'failed' },
138	        overrideAccess: true,
139	      })
140	      break
141	    }
142	
143	    case 'charge.refunded': {
144	      const paymentIntentId = event.data.object.payment_intent as string
145	
146	      const transactions = await payload.find({
147	        collection: 'transactions',
148	        where: {
149	          providerTransactionId: { equals: paymentIntentId },
150	        },
151	        limit: 1,
152	        depth: 0,
153	        overrideAccess: true,
154	      })
155	
156	      if (transactions.totalDocs === 0) {
157	        payload.logger.warn(
158	          { paymentIntentId },
159	          'Stripe webhook: transaction not found for charge.refunded',
160	        )
161	        return
162	      }
163	
164	      await payload.update({
165	        collection: 'transactions',
166	        id: transactions.docs[0].id,
167	        data: { status: 'refunded' },
168	        overrideAccess: true,
169	      })
170	      break
171	    }
172	
173	    default:
174	      // Unhandled event type — acknowledge without processing
175	      break
176	  }
177	}
178

1	/**
2	 * PayPal Webhook Handler
3	 *
4	 * POST /api/webhooks/paypal
5	 *
6	 * Verifies PayPal webhook signature, updates Transaction status, and grants
7	 * entitlements on successful payment. Always returns 200 to satisfy PayPal's
8	 * requirement for fast acknowledgment.
9	 *
10	 * @fileType api-route
11	 * @domain payments
12	 * @pattern webhook
13	 * @ai-summary Handles PayPal webhook events for payment confirmation and refunds
14	 */
15	
16	import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
17	
18	import { getPayload } from 'payload'
19	import config from '@payload-config'
20	
21	import { grantProductEntitlements } from '@/lib/payment/grant-entitlements'
22	import { verifyPayPalWebhook } from '@/lib/payment/paypal'
23	
24	interface PayPalWebhookResource {
25	  id: string
26	  supplementary_data?: {
27	    related_ids?: {
28	      order_id?: string
29	    }
30	  }
31	}
32	
33	interface PayPalWebhookEvent {
34	  event_type: string
35	  resource: PayPalWebhookResource
36	}
37	
38	export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
39	  const payload = await getPayload({ config })
40	
41	  // 1. Parse body as JSON
42	  let body: object
43	  try {
44	    body = await request.json()
45	  } catch {
46	    payload.logger.error('Failed to parse PayPal webhook body')
47	    return NextResponse.json({ received: true }, { status: 200 })
48	  }
49	
50	  // 2. Extract PayPal headers for signature verification
51	  const headers: Record<string, string> = {
52	    'paypal-transmission-id': request.headers.get('paypal-transmission-id') || '',
53	    'paypal-transmission-time': request.headers.get('paypal-transmission-time') || '',
54	    'paypal-transmission-sig': request.headers.get('paypal-transmission-sig') || '',
55	    'paypal-cert-url': request.headers.get('paypal-cert-url') || '',
56	    'paypal-auth-algo': request.headers.get('paypal-auth-algo') || '',
57	  }
58	
59	  // 3. Verify webhook signature
60	  try {
61	    const isValid = await verifyPayPalWebhook(body, headers)
62	    if (!isValid) {
63	      payload.logger.error('PayPal webhook signature verification failed')
64	      return NextResponse.json({ received: true }, { status: 200 })
65	    }
66	  } catch (err) {
67	    payload.logger.error({ error: err }, 'PayPal webhook signature verification error')
68	    return NextResponse.json({ received: true }, { status: 200 })
69	  }
70	
71	  // 4. Route event by type
72	  const event = body as PayPalWebhookEvent
73	  try {
74	    await handleEvent(payload, event)
75	  } catch (err) {
76	    payload.logger.error(
77	      { error: err, eventType: event.event_type },
78	      'PayPal webhook handler error',
79	    )
80	  }
81	
82	  return NextResponse.json({ received: true }, { status: 200 })
83	}
84	
85	async function handleEvent(
86	  payload: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getPayload>>,
87	  event: PayPalWebhookEvent,
88	): Promise<void> {
89	  switch (event.event_type) {
90	    case 'CHECKOUT.ORDER.APPROVED': {
91	      const orderId = event.resource.id
92	
93	      const transactions = await payload.find({
94	        collection: 'transactions',
95	        where: {
96	          providerTransactionId: { equals: orderId },
97	        },
98	        limit: 1,
99	        depth: 0,
100	        overrideAccess: true,
101	      })
102	
103	      if (transactions.totalDocs === 0) {
104	        payload.logger.warn({ orderId }, 'PayPal webhook: transaction not found')
105	        return
106	      }
107	
108	      const transaction = transactions.docs[0]
109	
110	      // Idempotency: skip if already succeeded
111	      if (transaction.status === 'succeeded') {
112	        return
113	      }
114	
115	      // Update status to succeeded
116	      await payload.update({
117	        collection: 'transactions',
118	        id: transaction.id,
119	        data: { status: 'succeeded' },
120	        overrideAccess: true,
121	      })
122	
123	      // Grant entitlements
124	      try {
125	        await grantProductEntitlements(
126	          transaction.user as string,
127	          transaction.product as string,
128	          transaction.id,
129	        )
130	      } catch (err) {
131	        payload.logger.error(
132	          { error: err, transactionId: transaction.id },
133	          'Failed to grant product entitlements',
134	        )
135	      }
136	      break
137	    }
138	
139	    case 'PAYMENT.CAPTURE.COMPLETED': {
140	      // The capture ID is in resource.id, but we need the order ID to find the transaction.
141	      // Try to get the order ID from supplementary_data.related_ids.order_id
142	      const orderId = event.resource.supplementary_data?.related_ids?.order_id || event.resource.id
143	
144	      const transactions = await payload.find({
145	        collection: 'transactions',
146	        where: {
147	          providerTransactionId: { equals: orderId },
148	        },
149	        limit: 1,
150	        depth: 0,
151	        overrideAccess: true,
152	      })
153	
154	      if (transactions.totalDocs === 0) {
155	        payload.logger.warn(
156	          { orderId },
157	          'PayPal webhook: transaction not found for PAYMENT.CAPTURE.COMPLETED',
158	        )
159	        return
160	      }
161	
162	      const transaction = transactions.docs[0]
163	
164	      // Idempotency guard: only process if still pending (skip if already succeeded, refunded, or failed)
165	      if (transaction.status !== 'pending') {
166	        return
167	      }
168	
169	      await payload.update({
170	        collection: 'transactions',
171	        id: transaction.id,
172	        data: { status: 'succeeded' },
173	        overrideAccess: true,
174	      })
175	      break
176	    }
177	
178	    case 'PAYMENT.CAPTURE.REFUNDED': {
179	      const captureId = event.resource.id
180	
181	      const transactions = await payload.find({
182	        collection: 'transactions',
183	        where: {
184	          providerTransactionId: { equals: captureId },
185	        },
186	        limit: 1,
187	        depth: 0,
188	        overrideAccess: true,
189	      })
190	
191	      if (transactions.totalDocs === 0) {
192	        payload.logger.warn(
193	          { captureId },
194	          'PayPal webhook: transaction not found for PAYMENT.CAPTURE.REFUNDED',
195	        )
196	        return
197	      }
198	
199	      await payload.update({
200	        collection: 'transactions',
201	        id: transactions.docs[0].id,
202	        data: { status: 'refunded' },
203	        overrideAccess: true,
204	      })

206	    }

1	/**
2	 * Transaction Refund Endpoint
3	 *
4	 * POST /api/admin/transactions/{id}/refund
5	 * Admin-only — calls refundStripe or refundPayPal and updates status to 'refunded'
6	 *
7	 * @fileType api-route
8	 * @domain payments
9	 */
10	
11	import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
12	import { getPayload } from 'payload'
13	
14	import config from '@payload-config'
15	import type { Transaction } from '@/payload-types'
16	import { refundStripe } from '@/lib/payment/stripe'
17	import { refundPayPal } from '@/lib/payment/paypal'
18	import { AccountRole } from '@/server/payload/collections/Users/roles'
19	
20	interface RouteParams {
21	  params: Promise<{ id: string }>
22	}
23	
24	export async function POST(req: NextRequest, { params }: RouteParams) {
25	  const { id } = await params
26	  const payload = await getPayload({ config })
27	
28	  // 1. Authenticate
29	  const authResult = await payload.auth({ headers: req.headers })
30	  if (!authResult.user) {
31	    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 })
32	  }
33	
34	  // 2. Check admin role
35	  if (
36	    !('collection' in authResult.user) ||
37	    authResult.user.collection !== 'users' ||
38	    authResult.user.role !== AccountRole.Admin
39	  ) {
40	    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Forbidden' }, { status: 403 })
41	  }
42	
43	  // 3. Fetch transaction
44	  let transaction: Transaction | null = null
45	  try {
46	    transaction = (await payload.findByID({
47	      collection: 'transactions',
48	      id,
49	      depth: 0,
50	      overrideAccess: true,
51	    })) as Transaction
52	  } catch (err) {
53	    // Handle both operational errors and not-found errors
54	    if (err instanceof Error && (err.name === 'NotFound' || err.message.includes('Not Found'))) {
55	      return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Transaction not found' }, { status: 404 })
56	    }
57	    throw err
58	  }
59	
60	  if (!transaction) {
61	    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Transaction not found' }, { status: 404 })
62	  }
63	
64	  // 4. Validate status — double-refund guard
65	  if (transaction.status === 'refunded') {
66	    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'העסקה כבר הוחזרה' }, { status: 400 })
67	  }
68	
69	  if (transaction.status !== 'succeeded') {
70	    return NextResponse.json(
71	      { error: 'Only succeeded transactions can be refunded' },
72	      { status: 400 },
73	    )
74	  }
75	
76	  // 5. Call provider refund
77	  const { provider, providerTransactionId, amount } = transaction as {
78	    provider: 'stripe' | 'paypal'
79	    providerTransactionId: string
80	    amount: number
81	  }
82	
83	  try {
84	    if (provider === 'stripe') {
85	      await refundStripe(providerTransactionId, amount)
86	    } else if (provider === 'paypal') {
87	      await refundPayPal(providerTransactionId, amount)
88	    } else {
89	      return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unknown payment provider' }, { status: 400 })
90	    }
91	  } catch (err) {
92	    payload.logger.error({ error: err, transactionId: id, provider }, 'Refund operation failed')
93	    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Refund operation failed' }, { status: 500 })
94	  }
95	
96	  // 6. Update status to refunded
97	  await payload.update({
98	    collection: 'transactions',
99	    id,
100	    data: { status: 'refunded' },
101	    overrideAccess: true,
102	  })
103	
104	  return NextResponse.json({ success: true })
105	}
106
